topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged, a small, insistent vibration against the smooth glass of my phone. My thumb hovered over the screen, a familiar, low-level hum of apprehension starting in my chest. It was a profile picture I recognized immediately: Mr. Harrison, my history teacher. My breath hitched, a shallow intake of air. I slowly navigated to his page, my eyes tracing the recent posts—a picture of a coffee shop, a casual comment about a historical event. A strange, heavy stillness settled over me, like wading through thick syrup. I felt my shoulders slump, the tension I’d been holding all morning simply dissolving into a heavy, unmoving weight. I didn't click anything. I just stared, the silence of my room amplifying the distant sound of traffic outside.

[story 2]

I kept my gaze fixed on my textbook, the worn cover a comforting anchor in the swirling confusion of my thoughts. Then, a notification broke the quiet. It was from Mr. Harrison. My heart gave a disconcerting little flutter, a quick, uneven rhythm against my ribs. I felt a strange reluctance to move, a desire to simply freeze in place. When I finally looked, the image of him smiling in a different light stared back at me from my feed. A wave of profound quiet washed over me, smoothing out the usual sharp edges of my anxiety. I didn't feel the urge to react, only a deep, passive acceptance of whatever this meant. My fingers rested flat on the desk, uncurling from any restless movement.

[story 3]

The screen lit up, pulling my attention away from the essay I was supposed to be revising. It was him. The realization hit not like a shockwave, but like the slow, steady settling of dust. I watched the feed scroll by, my movements becoming sluggish, as if moving through water. There was no immediate panic, just a profound sense of inertia. My jaw relaxed, the muscles softening under the pressure of this unexpected information. I didn't reach for the phone to close the application or to search for an explanation. Instead, I just let my eyes drift over the images, a vacant, almost passive observation. A strange calm enveloped me; the usual internal striving simply ceased.
